anglo_popular_state_religious_schools.name: YTA for Girls (Ramat Bet Shemesh branch)
YTA for Boys
anglo_popular_state_religious_schools.notes: English-language high school education in Jerusalem

ulpan_options.name: Government Ulpan (Misrad Haklita)
ulpan_options.notes: Free courses for new Olim immigrants

after_school_english_programs.name: A.H.A.V.A. - Jerusalem Area
after_school_english_programs.description: After-school English learning programs for Hebrew and English speakers, K-8th grade. Currently 8 classes in Jerusalem area

Jerusalem.special_education_english: Israel Elwyn offers preschool developmental centers and educational services for children with special needs. KESHER provides support groups with English, Hebrew, Arabic, and Russian-speaking staff. Sulam serves children with intellectual disabilities, motor dysfunction, and autism.
Jerusalem.notes: Jerusalem has the highest concentration of international schools in Israel. Multiple IB programs available. Strong support network for English-speaking families and special education services.

international_schools.name: Walworth Barbour American International School (WBAIS)
international_schools.location_in_city: Even Yehuda (20km north of Tel Aviv)

anglo_popular_state_religious_schools.name: Morasha Jewish Day School
anglo_popular_state_religious_schools.notes: Bilingual Hebrew-English education for expat families

ulpan_options.name: Ulpan Aviv - Tel Aviv Branch
ulpan_options.notes: Multiple locations in Tel Aviv with tailor-made programs

ulpan_options.name: Ulpan Bayit
ulpan_options.notes: Located in Florentin, uses Israeli media texts and poetry

ulpan_options.name: This Is Not An Ulpan (TINAU)
ulpan_options.notes: Hebrew and Arabic for all levels in Tel Aviv and Jerusalem

after_school_english_programs.name: Ahava English Programs - Tel Aviv Area
after_school_english_programs.description: After-school English learning for native and Hebrew speakers, K-8th grade

english_speaking_ganim.name: Wonder School
english_speaking_ganim.notes: One of Israel's most expensive kindergartens, bilingual Hebrew-English, selective enrollment

english_speaking_ganim.name: Rainbow Nursery
english_speaking_ganim.notes: Bilingual nursery in Tel Aviv, English and Hebrew programs

english_speaking_ganim.name: CityKids
english_speaking_ganim.notes: Ministry of Education approved nursery with 6 classrooms

Tel Aviv.special_education_english: Access to English-speaking special education services through major organizations. KESHER and Israel Elwyn have operations in Tel Aviv area.
Tel Aviv.notes: Major international school hub. Extensive English-speaking kindergarten options available. Multiple ulpan options for adults. Strong expat community support.

ulpan_options.name: Ulpan Bayit - Haifa Branch
ulpan_options.notes: Face-to-face classes and online options available

after_school_english_programs.name: Speak Up - Haifa
after_school_english_programs.description: Private English language school founded 2013, 450+ students weekly, native English teachers for group and private lessons

Haifa.special_education_english: Some English-speaking support available through general Israeli special education system
Haifa.notes: Fewer international schools compared to Jerusalem and Tel Aviv. Good private English language school options. Limited English-speaking kindergarten options documented.

anglo_popular_state_religious_schools.name: AMIT Noam Elementary
anglo_popular_state_religious_schools.notes: Located in Kfar Batya, Ra'anana. Strong Anglo community

Ra'anana.notes: Ra'anana is known for welcoming Anglo community and family-friendly atmosphere. Several state religious schools with some English-medium instruction. Data on international schools limited.

anglo_popular_state_religious_schools.name: Mr. and Mrs. Lester Sutker AMIT School for Boys
Midreshet AMIT Arts & Sciences Junior & Senior High School for Girls
Yachad Modiin High School
anglo_popular_state_religious_schools.notes: Founded 2005, largest school incorporating religious and secular students learning together

Modi'in.notes: Modi'in located midway between Jerusalem and Tel Aviv. Excellent transportation. Strong religious school options. Limited documented international schools.

anglo_popular_state_religious_schools.name: AMIT Bellows Ulpanat Noga High School
anglo_popular_state_religious_schools.notes: High school for girls in Beit Shemesh

Beit Shemesh.notes: Enormous range of school options including Dati Leumi, Haredi, and Anglo-oriented schools. Many schools teach partially in English. Growing Anglo community.

Herzliya.notes: TreeHouse has 5 campuses (TH1-TH5) serving ages 1-18. IB World School accreditation. Strong international community.

ulpan_options.name: Ulpan Israeli
ulpan_options.notes: Based in Netanya, multiple levels available. Customized group programs

ulpan_options.name: Ulpan Bayit
ulpan_options.notes: Face-to-face and online classes via Zoom

Netanya.notes: Primary hub for French-speaking community but lacks French school. Good ulpan options. Limited international school data documented.

Ashdod.notes: Part of French-speaking community hub. Emerging neighborhood attracting more expats. Limited international school documentation.

Ashkelon.notes: Southern coast city. Limited international school data available.

after_school_english_programs.name: Experiential School for English Language Studies
after_school_english_programs.description: English enrichment classes for children grades 2-8

after_school_english_programs.name: Berlitz Language School
after_school_english_programs.description: Private English language instruction, located in Kiryat Hamishla mall

Beer Sheva.special_education_english: Supported by Youth Futures mentoring programs for at-risk children
Beer Sheva.notes: Southern hub with educational disparities. No gifted classes documented. Growing support programs for disadvantaged populations.

Petah Tikva.notes: Limited international school and English program documentation available. Part of central Israel region.

anglo_popular_state_religious_schools.name: NAALE Amana - Kfar Saba
anglo_popular_state_religious_schools.notes: One of Israel's most prestigious high schools for religious girls

Kfar Saba.notes: Family-friendly city known for excellent schools and quiet residential areas. Limited international school documentation.

Rehovot.notes: Academic and scientific hub, home to Weizmann Institute. Limited international school documentation.

Rishon LeZion.notes: Rapidly growing city near Tel Aviv. Limited international school data documented.

Efrat.notes: Limited data available. Documented as having well-developed educational facilities.

anglo_popular_state_religious_schools.name: AMIT Ma'ale Adumim
anglo_popular_state_religious_schools.notes: Complete junior and senior high school, opened 2003, 360+ students

after_school_english_programs.name: A.H.A.V.A. English Programs
after_school_english_programs.description: After-school English programs with K and 1st-grade frontal classes when possible

Maale Adumim.notes: High quality of life with well-developed educational and recreational facilities. Growing English-speaking population with supporting programs.

anglo_popular_state_religious_schools.name: Ulpanat AMIT Givat Shmuel
anglo_popular_state_religious_schools.notes: High school for girls, 100% bagrut pass rate, ranked top school in city

Givat Shmuel.notes: Mix of secular and religious schools. Tichon Givat Shmuel is secular co-ed high school.

anglo_popular_state_religious_schools.name: AMIT Ginsburg Bar Ilan Gush Dan
anglo_popular_state_religious_schools.notes: Ranked top religious high school in Israel and eighth overall

Ramat Gan.notes: Ramat Gan hosts Ohel Shem, a secular high school with 1,550 students. Strong religious school options.

Givatayim.notes: 41 kindergartens, 9 elementary schools, 4 high schools. One of highest secondary school matriculation rates in Israel. Brenner School pioneered entrepreneurship programs.

international_schools.name: Collège-lycée franco-israélien Marcel et Lucette Drahi

Holon.notes: French school member of AEFE (French Education Abroad network). Limited English program documentation.

Bat Yam.notes: Ramot Comprehensive School offers entrepreneurship classes. Limited international school documentation.

Bnei Brak.notes: Haredi community city. Limited international or English-language school documentation available.

Ramat HaSharon.notes: City has 7 elementary schools, 2 middle schools, 2 high schools. Home to Rimon School of Jazz and Contemporary Music.

international_schools.name: Alexander Muss High School in Israel

Hod HaSharon.notes: Alexander Muss program for international students from US/Canada, 2 months to full semester. 12 elementary, 4 middle, 5 high schools documented.

Lod.notes: Limited documented data on international or English-language schools.

Ramla.notes: Limited documented data on international or English-language schools.

Nazareth.notes: K-12 Evangelical Christian school founded 1935-1936. Arab Christian schools include St. Joseph's Eclerical School and Nuns of St. Joseph School. About 50% of students attend Christian schools.

Acre.notes: Limited documented data on schools. Northern coastal city with historical significance.

Tiberias.notes: Historical Alliance Israélite Universelle schools used French as medium of instruction. Bar-Ilan University teaching hospitals located here.

Safed.notes: Two mamlachti elementary schools. Zefat Academic College operates in city. Received Education Prize from Ministry in 2012. Limited international school documentation.

Karmiel.notes: Galilee city. Limited documented data on international or English-language schools.

Nahariya.notes: Northern coastal city. Limited documented data on international or English-language schools.

Kiryat Shmona.notes: Northern city near Lebanese border. Limited documented data on schools. Experienced education disruptions.

Kiryat Ata.notes: Part of Krayot neighborhood in Haifa Bay area. Sci-Tech school available.

Kiryat Bialik.notes: Krayot neighborhood city. Sci-Tech College and technological school (college) available.

Kiryat Motzkin.notes: Krayot neighborhood. 18 schools, 11,000 students documented. Sci-Tech school available.

Kiryat Yam.notes: Krayot neighborhood. 15 preschools, 8 elementary, 3 high schools documented.

Yokneam.notes: Schools such as Tidhar and Hata'B Hadasha documented. Limited international school data.

Afula.notes: Technion's Rappaport Medical School has teaching hospitals here. Limited school documentation.

Beit She'an.notes: One of Israel's most earthquake-at-risk towns. Limited school documentation.

after_school_english_programs.name: Lunch and Learn Program
after_school_english_programs.description: Extended school day with nourishing hot lunch for 500 at-risk elementary students

Dimona.special_education_english: Youth Futures mentoring program assists at-risk children and families since 2006
Dimona.notes: Southern periphery city. No gifted classes documented. Known as robotics hub (World ORT Kadima Mada). Large immigrant population with support programs.

Arad.notes: Southern city with economic challenges. At-risk youth support programs available.

Sderot.notes: Southern periphery city. No gifted classes documented. At-risk youth support programs.

Ofakim.notes: Southern periphery city. No gifted classes documented. At-risk youth with support programs.

Kiryat Gat.notes: Rogozin Kiryat Gat Sci-Tech Vocational School available. Limited school documentation.

Nes Ziona.notes: 15 schools documented. Limited international or English-language school data.

Rosh HaAyin.notes: 21 schools documented. Rosh HaAyin College building identified. Limited international school documentation.

Shoham.notes: 13 schools documented. Limited international or English-language school data.

anglo_popular_state_religious_schools.name: Yeshiva schools and Seminaries (Haredi)
anglo_popular_state_religious_schools.notes: 30 elementary schools, 20 secondary schools including Mir Brachfeld yeshiva

Modi'in Illit.notes: Also known as Kiryat Sefer. Primarily Haredi community with extensive yeshiva system. Limited international school documentation.

Elad.notes: Ultra-Orthodox community city. Limited documented data on schools.
